title: SAFX heading towards 250% right now. Securing 50% profit! Nice. Lets break 150% next. Who on board on SAFX since -20%???
author: Jareo_San

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
Nice trade, congrats on locking in gains. Taking 50% off the table at +250% is solid risk management — way better than round-tripping a winner.

That said, after a move like this I’d be careful chasing momentum unless there’s a clear catalyst (earnings, guidance, contract news, etc.) backing the move. Big vertical runs often come with sharp pullbacks, especially if volume starts fading.

For anyone who got in around -20%, trimming into strength and letting a smaller runner ride with a stop isn’t a bad approach. Protect capital first, upside second.

Curious — what’s driving your conviction for 150%+ from here? Fundamentals improving, or mostly technical momentum?

replies.author: Jareo_San
replies.body: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/southern-energy-renewables-national-laboratory-123000664.html strong earning with merger discussion with proper chart navigation

replies.author: Jareo_San
replies.body: I wont be bother by other people momentum since i have a flexible opinion base on this company circumstances and capability

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body:
Congrats on locking in profits — always feels good to de-risk on a strong move. Just a gentle reminder though: after a +200% run, risk/reward changes fast. I’d be watching volume sustainability, order book liquidity, and whether there’s an actual catalyst behind this move versus pure momentum. Scaling out instead of all-in/all-out has saved me more than once.

For anyone still holding from the red days, what’s your thesis now? Are you playing continuation, or waiting for a pullback to re-enter? Curious how others are managing downside here, especially if BTC sneezes.

replies.author: Jareo_San
replies.body: Swing and up side trim to avoid any loss even its going above TP
